name: Haul Fleet Overload Policy Under Grade and Price Uncertainty
description: Array-instanced RAM/asset-decision model (HAUL_FLEET_MODEL_SPEC.md). A fleet of N haul trucks, each carrying its own cumulative-damage state, under a grade-and-price-conditional overload policy. Per-truck state is carried by array `expression` + vector-preserving `lag` (the stateless recurrence proven viable by the fleet-array spike). Fleet size = the `Fleet` dimension size (5 here for a Stage-2 demo; raise to 40 for the full deliverable). Two elements are flagged in their descriptions as REQUIRING engine item 3 (argmin_array) per WORKPLAN_FLEET_INSTANCING.md: `damage_penalized` and `overload_target`. Remove those two and hard-set `overload_target` to a fixed truck index to run the rest on today's engine.

elements.name: Load exponent (damage power law)
elements.description: The single largest model uncertainty (spec 1.1). A distribution, never a point value: a decision that flips between beta 2.5 and 3.5 is a request for better data.

elements.name: Grade threshold g*
elements.description: Overload only when face grade exceeds this (normalized). Optimization variable.

elements.name: Price threshold p*
elements.description: Overload only when price exceeds this. Optimization variable.

elements.name: Metal price
elements.description: Commodity price, sampled once per realization (constant within a run). This gives the cross-realization price uncertainty the policy frontier and tail-risk outputs need. A within-run stochastic price PATH (GBM return integrated through a stock, or an OU process carrying its level, or a historical bootstrap series) is a refinement — swap it in when intra-run price dynamics matter.

elements.name: Face ore grade (normalized 0-1)
elements.description: Grade sequence from the mine plan (spec 1.2): an input with uncertainty, not something WaSim computes.

elements.name: Overload active (fleet-wide gate this step)
elements.description: Grade-and-price-conditional overload policy: overload iff grade > g* AND price > p* (spec 1.2).

elements.name: Damage + failed-truck penalty (for dispatch)
elements.description: Penalty-masked-argmin idiom (WORKPLAN item 4): add BIG*(failed) so failed trucks never win the argmin below.

elements.name: Truck chosen to overload
elements.description: REQUIRES engine item 3 (argmin_array). Returns the 1-based index of the least-damaged available truck = the wear-levelling dispatch choice (spec 1.3). To run on today's engine, replace this element with a fixed value.

elements.name: Per-truck payload (t)
elements.description: 300t if this truck is the overload target and overload is on, else 240t nominal.

elements.name: Haul cycles per truck per step
elements.description: Aggregate cycle rate per truck per week (spec 3.4: model haulage as aggregate rates, not discrete trips through shovel queues).

elements.name: Per-truck damage rate
elements.description: Per-STEP power-law damage per truck: (1-failed)*(payload/240)^beta * cycles * scale. A failed truck accrues no more damage. Scale 0.00016 sets nominal (240t, beta=3) life to ~1.0 over ~156 weeks (3 yr) of full-time hauling; an overloaded (300t) truck at beta=3 wears (1.25)^3 ~ 1.95x faster. Calibrate to real S-N / L10 data.

elements.name: Damage (previous step)
elements.description: Vector-preserving one-step lag of `damage` (engine item 2, landed). Breaks the accumulation cycle so each truck integrates its own damage.

elements.name: Per-truck cumulative damage
elements.description: Miner's-rule cumulative damage per truck; fails at 1.0. `damage_rate` is already per-step damage, so the recurrence is a plain sum (no dt multiply — dt is folded into the rate's scale). Each member records to damage#1..#N (engine item 1, landed) — the raw damage-spread output.

elements.name: Per-truck failed flag
elements.description: Per-truck failure as an array-valued `status` latch (engine item 5 / Option B): member i sets when its damage crosses 1.0 and HOLDS thereafter, independently per truck. The set condition reads `damage_prev[i]` (one-step-delayed) to break the failed->damage_rate->damage->failed cycle; the latch's own persisted state supplies the 'stays failed' memory. The reset condition is a constant-false (literal 0) = no repair yet — to add repair-and-return, point `reset` at a per-member repair-completion condition.

elements.name: Failed (previous step)
elements.description: Vector-preserving lag of the `failed` latch, used only to count newly-failed trucks this step (new_failures).

elements.name: Payload of available trucks
elements.description: payload masked by availability, summed downstream for fleet tonnage.

elements.name: Available trucks
elements.description: N minus number failed — the availability that closes the reinforcing feedback loop (spec 1.4).

elements.name: Fleet tonnage rate
elements.description: Total tonnes/step = cycles * sum of available-truck payloads. Falls as trucks fail — the loop's forcing term.

elements.name: Cumulative metal produced
elements.description: Metal = tonnage * grade * recovery(0.85). Damage is paid in tonnes but revenue is earned in metal (spec 1.2).

elements.name: New failures this step
elements.description: Count of trucks that newly transitioned to failed this step.

elements.name: Cumulative maintenance cost
elements.description: $500k per major failure event (structural/drivetrain overhaul), amortized into the step rate.

elements.name: Net value (revenue - maintenance)
elements.description: Undiscounted net-value proxy (swap in pv_factor for true NPV). The optimization objective.

elements.name: Damage spread (max - min)
elements.description: The wear-levelling diagnostic: 0 = perfectly balanced fleet, large = a few trucks aging fast toward clustered failure. Naive dispatch grows this; wear-levelling shrinks it (spec 2.4).
